Hey guys, JB here in the house. Time for your throwback Thursday. I have an awesome clip for you today. It's going to be on a subject that I haven't spoken about in a while. Let's cut to it right now. Have a great day and we'll talk in tomorrow. The way you lower action threshold is this. Typically it's this. Some sort of guarantee. That's why when you see all these infomercials, what are they always doing? You use the money back and say at the beginning, you don't lower action threshold in the beginning. You lower it at the end. When someone's close but not quite there, you temporarily lower their action threshold by saying, and guess what? If I'm wrong, you can give it back. It's not everything I said. You can return it and get a refund. That's how you lower action threshold. In the stock market, you can't do that typically. I guess in some investments you can, for the most part, you can't. The way you lower action threshold is as follows. I mentioned it before, but I want to touch on it a bit more intensely now. You make your clients run a different movie. Let me explain to you what that means. Remember I said to you that I was walking through the airport. I saw the guy take a swing of the golf club. Me, the low action threshold, I run the movie that says, oh my God, I can imagine myself on the 17th, green or Augusta, just playing the amazing round the golf. All the wonderful things. I'm a positive thinking guy. I think of all the great stuff. That's the movie. I actually have pictures. I run a movie. I see myself teeing up on the 18th. Wham. Feels good. I don't go to the run a movie that says, oh my God, if I'm wrong, I'll feel stupid. I remember when I was, I once bought a thing, my wife said I'm an idiot about that and I felt short of money and they wouldn't return it when I called for the return. I don't do that stuff. I'm like, ah, it's bad. I don't run the negative movie. My father, on the other hand, will run a movie of disaster. If I'm wrong, oh my God, I'll feel like an idiot. I'll be taking this. Then I'll have to be on the phone trying to return the product. They'll probably screw me for that. Then my wife will be mad at me and I'll be in a whole, terrible, terrible, exaggerated downside. Then when he runs the upside, he says, yeah, I'll probably take it to the thing. It'll suck. Probably won't even worry. He runs a very negative upside film. Everyone get that? That's only the high act threshold. They run a negative, a long window negative movie and when they project out into the future, they run a very short movie without a lot of emotional movement. They don't get moved emotionally. Does that make sense? Yes? What you do to lower action threshold is you make them run a different movie. The way you typically do it is say, let me ask you a question. What's the worst that can possibly happen here? Let's say I'm wrong and the stop drops 10% and you lose $4,000. It's not going to put you into poor house. They'll say, no. Probably say exactly. Let's say I'm right. It goes up 20 points and you make 20,000. It's not going to make me the richest man, will it? They'll say, no. Say exactly, but what it will do, it will serve as a benchmark for future business. It will show you that I'm a guy that's going to put you into the market at the right time and more importantly, take you out. It'll serve to build our track record. We're going to base it this time. Next time I'll let you do another base hit with me and then one more base hit, but after three base hits together, I'm going to ask you if you're going to demand that you step up to the plate and let a home run together. Listen, I plan you, that's the beginning, how you actually make them future pace a different movie about if they're wrong, the consequences are very minor, but if you're right, you say, by the way, what I could do for you besides this one trade over the long term, the way of new issues, IPOs, warrant conversions, investment backing deals. I want to be an asset to you and your family. You start piling on the long term benefits. You blunt the short term movie and then you say, why don't we do this? Instead of taking 50,000, start off small. The firm minimum is $25,000. All I'm asking for is that you can be one shot, very, very small, work with the stuff and you actually make them run a different way. Give me a shot and believe me, you will not be sorry.
